21.reason: Unencrypted FTP - credentials sent in plaintext

22.reason: SSH exposed - low risk if patched, monitor for brute force

23.reason: Telnet is unencrypted and obsolete - high compromise risk

139.reason: NetBIOS - legacy Windows file sharing, exploitable

445.reason: SMB - historically the most exploited port (WannaCry, NotPetya class attacks)

1433.reason: MSSQL exposed - database should never be internet-facing

1521.reason: Oracle DB exposed - critical data exposure risk

3306.reason: MySQL exposed - database should never be internet-facing

3389.reason: RDP - common brute-force and ransomware entry point

5432.reason: PostgreSQL exposed - database should never be internet-facing

5900.reason: VNC - remote screen access, often weak/no authentication

5984.reason: CouchDB - no auth by default, full data exposure risk

6379.reason: Redis - no auth by default, complete data exposure risk

9200.reason: Elasticsearch - indexed data often readable without auth

27017.reason: MongoDB - no auth by default in older versions

8080.reason: Alternate HTTP - low risk if intended, verify it's expected

8443.reason: Alternate HTTPS - low risk if intended, verify it's expected

Windows 10/11.reason: Modern OS, ensure auto-updates are enabled

Linux (generic).reason: Verify this device receives regular security patches

macOS/iOS.reason: Modern OS, ensure auto-updates are enabled

Android.reason: Mobile OS - verify app permissions and OS update status

Embedded/IoT (Linux-based).reason: IoT devices are frequently unpatched and rarely updated by owners

Router/Network device.reason: Router firmware should be kept current - check manufacturer site for updates

IoT.reason: IoT devices have a historically poor security update track record

Router.reason: A compromised router can expose your entire network

Printer.reason: Printers are often overlooked and unpatched - common lateral movement target

Smart TV.reason: Smart TVs run apps with internet access and are rarely security-audited

Gaming Console.reason: Gaming consoles have network exposure - keep firmware updated

untrusted_unknown_vendor.reason: Vendor not identified - common for phones with MAC privacy randomization, or may indicate an unrecognized device

new_device_24h.reason: Recently joined network - verify this device is authorized
